title: The One in Massapequa

jokes.text: Did he put a little starch in your bloomers?
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions meeting Parker at the dry cleaners
jokes.punchline: Monica makes a sexual innuendo using dry cleaning terminology
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that connects dry cleaning to sexual innuendo with good economy

jokes.text: Who said that?
jokes.setup: Monica's inappropriate bloomers comment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's confused/shocked reaction to the innuendo
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that works but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: And he treats me like a queen. Except at night, when he treats me like the naughty girl I am.
jokes.setup: Romantic description of how Parker treats her
jokes.punchline: Sudden turn to oversharing about their sexual dynamic
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe oversharing moment with good structural turn

jokes.text: Hopefully this time Mom won't boo you.
jokes.setup: Monica wanting to give the toast again after what happened at their 20th
jokes.punchline: Reveals their mother actually booed Monica's previous toast
jokes.explanation: Efficiently reveals backstory while showing family dynamics and Monica's determination

jokes.text: You wonder why Ross is their favorite?
jokes.setup: Ross's successful toasts versus Monica's disasters
jokes.punchline: Monica's bitter acknowledgment of the obvious favoritism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness with Ross, economically delivered

jokes.text: You know what they're gonna say this year? "God, you."
jokes.setup: Monica describing how people praise Ross after his toasts
jokes.punchline: Her confidence that this year they'll praise her instead
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven confidence that sets up future failure

jokes.text: Really? You can do that? - Oh, you kidding me? Watch.
jokes.setup: Joey claims he can cry on cue as an actor
jokes.punchline: Sets up his inability to perform when watched
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Joey's acting incompetence

jokes.text: Well, I can't do it with you guys watching me.
jokes.setup: Joey confidently claiming he can cry on cue
jokes.punchline: Immediate failure and excuse-making
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey incompetence with believable excuse-making

jokes.text: Or as I like to call it: "Sob-fest 2002."
jokes.setup: Monica working on her toast for the anniversary party
jokes.punchline: Her overconfident nickname for her planned emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that reveals Monica's calculated approach to emotion

jokes.text: You're gonna talk about their dead pet?
jokes.setup: Monica showing a picture of Chi-Chi and calling it 'good stuff'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's horrified realization of Monica's tactless strategy
jokes.explanation: Efficient reaction that highlights the inappropriate nature of Monica's plan

jokes.text: And I got them Kama Sutra for the Elderly.
jokes.setup: Joey giving sweet gift of naming a star after the anniversary couple
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual gift for elderly parents
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey inappropriateness with good contrast to his sweet gesture

jokes.text: And I think my testicles may be in here too.
jokes.setup: Monica listing various types of ribbon in excessive detail
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating comment about his masculinity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler self-deprecation about being emasculated by domestic tasks

jokes.text: You were the 200-pounder who rode her.
jokes.setup: Monica complaining she couldn't get braces because of Chi-Chi's knee surgery
jokes.punchline: Ross revealing Monica caused the dog's injury by riding her
jokes.explanation: Excellent revelation that recontextualizes the story and pokes at Monica's childhood

jokes.text: Chandler, I'm kidding. Already you're my favorite.
jokes.setup: Parker pretending not to know Chandler's name
jokes.punchline: Immediate charm offensive declaring Chandler his favorite
jokes.explanation: Shows Parker's excessive charm and people-pleasing nature

jokes.text: Classic Ross.
jokes.setup: Ross saying they should get going to the party
jokes.punchline: Parker treating a normal statement as characteristically Ross-like
jokes.explanation: Efficiently shows Parker's tendency to find everything delightful and meaningful

jokes.text: Well, at least you were lucky to have him. Bow-wow, old friend. Bow-wow.
jokes.setup: Monica mentioning her old dog passed away
jokes.punchline: Parker's overly sentimental and childish tribute to a stranger's dead dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Parker's excessive sentimentality about everything

jokes.text: Well, there is an Arby's in the shape of a tepee.
jokes.setup: Parker asking if Massapequa is steeped in Native American history
jokes.punchline: Ross's dry response about the only 'Native American' landmark being a fast food restaurant
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan deflation of Parker's romantic notions with specific, absurd detail

jokes.text: We're driving? - Yeah. - Aces.
jokes.setup: Learning they're driving to Long Island
jokes.punchline: Parker's enthusiastic response to mundane transportation method
jokes.explanation: Shows Parker's ability to find excitement in anything, including car rides

jokes.text: You want to hang back and take a cab? - Okay, otherwise I'm not going.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Parker seems like a nice guy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate preference to avoid the car ride with Parker
jokes.explanation: Chandler's typical avoidance behavior and dry honesty about Parker

jokes.text: Jack? - Why serve food on such a sharp stick?
jokes.setup: Rachel asking for pearls of wisdom from 35 years of marriage
jokes.punchline: Jack's completely irrelevant observation about hors d'oeuvres
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack Geller non-sequitur that avoids the emotional question

jokes.text: It's a good question, Dad. It's a good question.
jokes.setup: Jack's bizarre comment about serving food on sharp sticks
jokes.punchline: Ross's diplomatic but clearly patronizing response to his father's randomness
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross trying to be respectful while managing his father's oddness

jokes.text: Son, I had to shave my ears for tonight. You can do this.
jokes.setup: Ross complaining about having to pretend to be married
jokes.punchline: Jack's comparison of pretending to be married to the sacrifice of ear grooming
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack Geller moment - absurd comparison delivered with paternal authority

jokes.text: If you're gonna do the ears, you may as well take a pass at the nose area.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning he had to shave his ears
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical grooming advice about Jack's nose hair
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler observation humor about practical but uncomfortable topics

jokes.text: Unbelievable. It's great. - I love marriage.
jokes.setup: Being asked how marriage is treating them when they're pretending to be married
jokes.punchline: Their overly enthusiastic but hollow praise for marriage
jokes.explanation: Good irony as they fake enthusiasm for a marriage that doesn't exist

jokes.text: The birthdays, the proms, the mitzvahs, both bar and bat.
jokes.setup: Parker rhapsodizing about all the good times in Event Room C
jokes.punchline: His detailed categorization of mitzvah types shows his over-enthusiasm for mundane details
jokes.explanation: Shows Parker's tendency to find deep meaning in generic party venues

jokes.text: I don't think the flash went off.
jokes.setup: Parker taking a 'mental picture' of everyone with a click sound
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about the imaginary camera malfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan that punctures Parker's pretentious moment

jokes.text: Somewhere, there is someone with a tranquilizer gun and a huge butterfly net looking for that man.
jokes.setup: Parker's overwhelming enthusiasm and positivity
jokes.punchline: Monica's suggestion that he belongs in a mental institution
jokes.explanation: Vivid imagery that captures everyone's feeling about Parker's intensity

jokes.text: I have to go too, but I don't want him complimenting my thing.
jokes.setup: Parker and Chandler going to find the men's room
jokes.punchline: Joey's fear of Parker's excessive positivity extending to bathroom situations
jokes.explanation: Funny extension of Parker's character to uncomfortable intimate situations

jokes.text: He called the Long Island Expressway a concrete miracle.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Parker's relentless enthusiasm during the car ride
jokes.punchline: Specific example of his ability to find wonder in mundane infrastructure
jokes.explanation: Perfect specific example of Parker's excessive positivity with great phrase choice

jokes.text: That waiter. His shoes. I must take a mental picture. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Parker continuing his pattern of finding everything amazing
jokes.punchline: His focus shifting to increasingly mundane details like a waiter's footwear
jokes.explanation: Escalates Parker's character trait to absurd specificity

jokes.text: In my opinion, your collective dating record reads like a who's who of human crap.
jokes.setup: Friends criticizing Parker and Phoebe defending him by comparing to their dating history
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's brutal assessment of everyone's romantic choices
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe moment - defending Parker while brutally roasting everyone else

jokes.text: What was wrong with Mona?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's harsh criticism of everyone's dating history including Mona
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive focus on just one ex instead of the bigger picture
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross missing the point and focusing on defending one relationship

jokes.text: And Stevie Wonder sang "Isn't She Lovely" as I walked down the aisle.
jokes.setup: Rachel describing their fake wedding as beautiful and spectacular
jokes.punchline: Absurdly over-the-top celebrity appearance at their fictional wedding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake wedding story into celebrity territory

jokes.text: You wouldn't think that Annie Leibovitz would forget to put film in the camera.
jokes.setup: Aunt asking to see wedding pictures of their elaborate fake wedding
jokes.punchline: Rachel's excuse involving a famous photographer making an amateur mistake
jokes.explanation: Clever excuse that maintains the elaborate lie while explaining lack of evidence

jokes.text: Okay, Ross, it has to be realistic.
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting Rachel rode in on a Harley for their fake wedding
jokes.punchline: Rachel calling for realism after describing Stevie Wonder and Annie Leibovitz
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment as Rachel draws arbitrary line about realism

jokes.text: What an inspired solution to man's plate-dispensing problems.
jokes.setup: Parker noticing a spring-loaded plate dispenser at the buffet
jokes.punchline: His grandiose description of a simple cafeteria mechanism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Parker moment finding profound meaning in mundane cafeteria equipment

jokes.text: Why don't you just try one? - Nah, they look too weird.
jokes.setup: Parker forcing Rachel to eat an oyster and describing the experience
jokes.punchline: Parker refusing to try one himself because they look weird
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy - Parker's enthusiasm doesn't extend to personal risk

jokes.text: You know, if you want to, I could just hold them down and you could just:
jokes.setup: Monica planning to make her parents cry with her toast
jokes.punchline: Joey offering to physically restrain them for emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation of Monica's plan with Joey's typically inappropriate helpfulness

jokes.text: Well, not at first, but it was intricate work.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning her wedding veil was made by blind Belgian nuns
jokes.punchline: Revealing the nuns went blind from making her fictional veil
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation that makes Rachel's fake wedding increasingly elaborate and problematic

jokes.text: But there were some people that said I looked like a floating angel.
jokes.setup: Rachel being modest about her appearance at the fake wedding
jokes.punchline: Her 'modest' description is actually quite grandiose
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Rachel's fake humility about the elaborate wedding

jokes.text: And the ring was the size of my fist.
jokes.setup: Ross describing the elaborate fake proposal story
jokes.punchline: The engagement ring being comically oversized
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation capping off the elaborate proposal story

jokes.text: Let's call a spade a spade. This party stinks.
jokes.setup: Phoebe deciding to kill Parker because he's too positive about everything
jokes.punchline: Her suddenly negative assessment of the party Parker loves
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's character turn from defending Parker to agreeing with everyone else

jokes.text: Are you sure it wasn't an oyster?
jokes.setup: Rachel describing slipping on a giant booger at the buffet
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical suggestion connecting to the oyster scene
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes Rachel's disgusting experience

jokes.text: Are you kidding? People acting like animals to music? Come on.
jokes.setup: Parker expressing love for the Bunny Hop dance
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's cynical but accurate description of group dancing
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's newfound cynicism perfectly deflating the innocent joy of group dancing

jokes.text: [GUESTS GROAN]
jokes.setup: Monica announcing she's giving the toast instead of Ross
jokes.punchline: Audible groaning from guests who remember her previous disasters
jokes.explanation: Perfect audience reaction that confirms Monica's reputation for bad toasts

jokes.text: As is our dog, Chi-Chi.
jokes.setup: Monica's toast mentioning deceased grandmother
jokes.punchline: Adding the dead dog to the toast with equal gravity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - treating a pet's death with inappropriate solemnity

jokes.text: Do me a favor, pass this to my parents. Remember, um, she's dead, okay? Her and Nana, gone.
jokes.setup: Monica showing the picture of Chi-Chi during her toast
jokes.punchline: Her awkward instruction to remember that both subjects are deceased
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward moment showing Monica's complete miscalculation of appropriate emotion

jokes.text: You people are made of stone.
jokes.setup: Monica's increasingly desperate attempts to make people cry with tragic references
jokes.punchline: Her frustrated accusation when the audience doesn't respond emotionally
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Monica's toast disaster - blaming the audience for her failure

jokes.text: Here's to Mom and Dad. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Monica's complete failure to move the audience with her emotional toast
jokes.punchline: Her defeated, casual ending to what was supposed to be a meaningful toast
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflated ending that captures Monica's complete surrender and disappointment

jokes.text: Why don't I remember this dog?
jokes.setup: Monica's entire emotional toast centered around Chi-Chi the dog
jokes.punchline: Jack not even remembering the dog Monica made central to her speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncture of Monica's emotional manipulation - the dog wasn't even memorable

jokes.text: I just wish Nana were alive to hear Ross' toast.
jokes.setup: Ross giving an effortlessly moving toast after Monica's disaster
jokes.punchline: Judy invoking dead Nana for Ross while ignoring Monica did the same thing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - same reference works for Ross but failed for Monica

jokes.text: Can I, um, get you something to drink, like a water and Valium?
jokes.setup: Parker gushing about the well-lit hallway
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggesting medication to calm his excessive enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phoebe's frustration with specific, practical solution

jokes.text: They were just brake lights, Parker.
jokes.setup: Parker presumably describing traffic as magical or meaningful
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's brutal deflation of his romantic interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of Phoebe completely shutting down Parker's manufactured wonder

jokes.text: You are like Santa Claus on Prozac. At Disneyland, getting laid.
jokes.setup: Phoebe calling herself a positive person compared to Parker
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly elaborate description of Parker's excessive happiness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating metaphor that perfectly captures Parker's overwhelming positivity

jokes.text: Hey. Don't let the best door in the world hit you in the ass on your way out.
jokes.setup: Parker leaving after their fight
jokes.punchline: Phoebe sarcastically mimicking his excessive enthusiasm even in anger
jokes.explanation: Excellent twist on common phrase that maintains Parker's speech pattern while being insulting

jokes.text: Isn't this the most incredible fight you've ever had in your entire life?
jokes.setup: Parker returning after their breakup fight
jokes.punchline: His inability to stop being positive even about their relationship ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Parker moment - even their breakup becomes the most amazing experience ever

jokes.text: But having a dove place the ring on your finger would've been no problem.
jokes.setup: Ross saying the sidecar wouldn't have been feasible
jokes.punchline: Rachel pointing out the dove was apparently feasible in their fake story
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the arbitrary nature of their fictional wedding details

jokes.text: Yeah, and it was the easiest 400 bucks I ever made.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying it was fun being married to him tonight
jokes.punchline: Ross treating their fake marriage like a paying gig
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of the romantic moment with practical Ross logic

jokes.text: [SOBBING] Oh, good God, Ross, how the hell do you do it?
jokes.setup: Monica finally crying after hearing Ross's story about their parents and Nana
jokes.punchline: Her frustrated sobbing acknowledgment of Ross's effortless emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff showing Monica finally experiencing what she tried to create, while acknowledging Ross's superior skill

tmdb_overview: The group attends the Gellers' 35th wedding anniversary. Ross and Rachel pretend to be married while attending the party.
